response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with genuine geospatial experience (founded GISCollective, built GIS mapping applications for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers at Dunnhumby). However, modern C++ exposure is limited and dated — a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project — with no Rust experience, and recent years have been spent in TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems programming. There is no direct routing or navigation pipeline experience, so the fit hinges on whether transferable systems skills outweigh the C++/Rust gap.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and GIS work for Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/second
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker/Kubernetes, and demonstrated willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No routing, tiling, or navigation guidance systems background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
